steps.title: Find the growth factor per 3 minutes
steps.content:
We have N(m) = 65 Q^{m/6}. After 3 minutes, m → m+3.
$$\frac{N(m+3)}{N(m)} = \frac{65 Q^{(m+3)/6}}{65 Q^{m/6}} = Q^{(m+3)/6 - m/6} = Q^{3/6} = Q^{1/2}.$$
Thus the population multiplies by $Q^{1/2}$ every 180 seconds.

steps.title: Convert growth factor to percent increase
steps.content:
A p% increase means the new amount is $(1 + \frac{p}{100})$ times the original. Set this equal to the growth factor:
$$1 + \frac{p}{100} = Q^{1/2}.$$
Solve for p:
$$\frac{p}{100} = Q^{1/2} - 1 \quad \Longrightarrow \quad p = 100\left(Q^{1/2} - 1\right).$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
